text:
in the past month, i've had dozens of conversations about the future with people who are definitively on the frontier.

just wanted to share some of these insights with y'all, in no particular order:

1. the bottleneck to applied AI isn't technical skill. it's imagination. most

the biggest AI opportunity right now is mid-market companies ($5M-$50M+ revenue):  

• too big for cookie-cutter solutions 
• too small for enterprise consulting 
• drowning in manual workflows 
• have budget but zero AI expertise  

they’re stuck with serious cash but frozen

in 2026, 

every company will be looking for “ai nerds”

not data scientists. not ml engineers. just people who know how to automate boring shit with claude code, ai and llms

the new valuable skill is knowing which tasks to automate and how to glue tools together

go to grok and paste a profile url.   

ask it “scrape tweets above 1k likes about [keyword]” for any account in your niche.  

grab 5-10 of yours plus 5-10 from 3 top accounts in your space.  

now build a context profile:  

• paste all tweets into claude  
• ask it to find

Leaders have to rebuild their intuitions

"A lot of leaders have built intuitions over 10 or 15 years, and they are highly regarded for those intuitions. But now with AI in the picture, those intuitions will have to be relearned. And leaders have to be vulnerable to do that. 

I https://t.co/gsmp6ARL9O

- go to n8n templates and filter by most recent
- download 10-15 workflow JSONs with their descriptions
- upload everything into a Claude project
- prompt
